tagline: A gamified way to prepare for the Life in the UK Test — and to know when you're actually ready.
why: Preparing for the Life in the UK Test means memorising a sprawl of facts with no clear sense of when you've done enough. I built Brit Ready to fix both halves of that: make the learning feel like a game rather than a slog, and give an honest readiness signal so you only book the real exam once you'll pass it. It's an unofficial study aid, built by someone who went through it.
how: It's a Next.js PWA styled with Tailwind CSS, with Zustand holding the learning and progress state on the client so everything works offline once installed. Questions mirror the real exam format, and a readiness model tracks your accuracy across topics to tell you when you're prepared.

features.title: Learn every fact
features.description: The full body of test material, broken into bite-sized, gamified sessions instead of a wall of text.

features.title: Real exam format
features.description: Practise under the same question style and timing as the official test, so the real thing feels familiar.

features.title: Readiness signal
features.description: Tracks your accuracy across topics and tells you, honestly, when you're ready to book.
